---
title: "hrcbd"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(broom)

# SIMULATION FUNCTIONS

### 1. Homoscedastic RCBD Simulation Model
R2_sim_het <- function(blocks,
                       treatments,
                       mu,
                       sigma_b,
                       R2_target,
                       response_type = "normal") {

  # Generate design matrix for a Randomized Complete Block Design (RCBD)
  data <- expand.grid(
    block = factor(1:blocks),
    treatment = factor(1:treatments)
  )

  # Simulation parameters under the Null Hypothesis (no treatment effect)
  alpha_t <- rep(0, treatments)
  beta_b <- rnorm(blocks, 0, sigma_b) # Random block effects

  # Calculate sum of squares for blocks to calibrate target R-squared
  S <- treatments * sum((beta_b - mean(beta_b))^2)

  # Homoscedastic baseline variance
  Vbar <- 1

  # Degrees of freedom allocations used for calculating target error variance
  A1 <- treatments + blocks - 2
  B1 <- treatments * blocks - 1

  # Derive error standard deviation conditional on target R-squared
  sigma <- sqrt(S * (1 - R2_target) /(Vbar * (R2_target * B1 - A1)))

  # Construct true underlying linear predictor
  mu_tb <- mu + alpha_t[data$treatment] + beta_b[data$block]

  V_tb <- 1

  # Generate homoscedastic error term
  e <- rnorm(nrow(data), mean = 0, sd = sigma * sqrt(V_tb))
  y1 <- mu_tb + e

  # Taylor series approximation variables for Log-normal mapping transformations
  Var_tb <- sigma^2 * V_tb
  a_tb <- sqrt(log(1 + Var_tb / mu_tb^2)) / sqrt(Var_tb)
  b_tb <- log(mu_tb) - a_tb * mu_tb - (a_tb^2 * Var_tb) / 2

  # Apply targeted data distribution transformations
  if (response_type == "normal") {
    response <- y1
  } else if (response_type == "lognormal") {
    response <- exp(a_tb * y1 + b_tb)
  } else if (response_type == "log") {
    response <- a_tb * y1 + b_tb
  } else if (response_type == "exp") {
    response <- exp(y1)
  }

  data$response <- response

  # Fit standard additive Two-Way ANOVA Model
  model <- aov(response ~ treatment + block, data)
  anova_tbl <- anova(model) |> tidy()

  treatment_row <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "treatment")

  F_stat <- treatment_row$statistic
  p_value <- treatment_row$p.value

  # Track empirical Type I Error rates (alpha = 0.05)
  reject_F <- p_value < 0.05

  # Track Family-Wise Error Rate (FWER) via Tukey's HSD pairwise adjustments
  tukey_reject <- TukeyHSD(model, "treatment")$treatment |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    pull(`p adj`) |>
    (\(x) any(x < 0.05))()

  R2 <- summary(lm(response ~ treatment + block, data))$r.squared

  # Extract Mean Squares for distribution analysis
  MS_error <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "Residuals") |>
    pull(meansq)
  
  MS_treatment <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "treatment") |>
    pull(meansq)

  tibble(
    F_stat = F_stat,
    reject_F = reject_F,
    R2 = R2,
    tukey_reject = tukey_reject,
    MSE = MS_error,
    MSa = MS_treatment
  )
}
```

```{r}
# EXPERIMENT 1: HOMOSCEDASTIC SIMULATIONS & EVALUATION

# Seed locked for exact pseudo-random number reproduction
set.seed(100)
R2_target <- 0.5

# Monte Carlo iterations (N = 10,000 per transformation type)
results_normal    <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_het(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "normal"))
results_lognormal <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_het(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "lognormal"))
results_log       <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_het(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "log"))
results_exp       <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_het(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "exp"))

# Aggregate operating performance characteristics across transformations
results <- bind_rows(
  normal = results_normal,
  lognormal = results_lognormal,
  log = results_log,
  exp = results_exp,
  .id = "dataset"
) |>
  group_by(dataset) |>
  summarise(
    type1_error = mean(reject_F), # Empirical Type I error rate
    mean_F = mean(F_stat),
    var_F = var(F_stat),
    mean_MSa = mean(MSa),
    var_MSa = var(MSa),
    mean_MSE = mean(MSE),
    var_MSE = var(MSE),
    mean_R2 = mean(R2),
    tukey_fwer = mean(tukey_reject) # Family-wise error rate inflation assessment
  )
```


```{r}
# Plotting Empirical Type I Error Rates for Homoscedastic Data Structures
ggplot(results, aes(x = dataset, y = type1_error)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "red3") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed") + # Nominal alpha threshold
  labs(
    x = "Transformation",
    y = "Type I Error Rate",
    title = "Type I Error Rate for Homoscedastic Data With Transformations"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 0.08) +
  theme_bw()
```


```{r}
### 2. Heteroscedastic Simulation Model (Variance Proportional to Squared Mean)
R2_sim_mu2 <- function(blocks,
                       treatments,
                       mu,
                       sigma_b,
                       R2_target,
                       response_type = "normal") {

  data <- expand.grid(
    block = factor(1:blocks),
    treatment = factor(1:treatments)
  )

  alpha_t <- rep(0, treatments)
  beta_b <- rnorm(blocks, 0, sigma_b)

  S <- treatments * sum((beta_b - mean(beta_b))^2)

  # CRITICAL STRUCTURAL CHANGE: Variance weight is defined as mean^2 matrix
  mean_matrix <- outer(
    alpha_t,
    beta_b,
    function(alpha, beta) mu + alpha + beta
  )
  variance_matrix <- mean_matrix^2
  Vbar <- mean(variance_matrix)

  A1 <- treatments + blocks - 2
  B1 <- treatments * blocks - 1

  sigma <- sqrt(S * (1 - R2_target) /(Vbar * (R2_target * B1 - A1)))
  mu_tb <- mu + alpha_t[data$treatment] + beta_b[data$block]

  # Assign variance proportional to cell expectation squared
  V_tb <- mu_tb^2

  # Error term generation containing cell-specific heteroscedastic weights
  e <- rnorm(nrow(data), mean = 0, sd = sigma * sqrt(V_tb))
  y1 <- mu_tb + e

  Var_tb <- sigma^2 * V_tb
  a_tb <- sqrt(log(1 + Var_tb / mu_tb^2)) / sqrt(Var_tb)
  b_tb <- log(mu_tb) - a_tb * mu_tb - (a_tb^2 * Var_tb) / 2

  if (response_type == "normal") {
    response <- y1
  } else if (response_type == "lognormal") {
    response <- exp(a_tb * y1 + b_tb)
  } else if (response_type == "log") {
    response <- a_tb * y1 + b_tb
  } else if (response_type == "exp") {
    response <- exp(y1)
  }

  data$response <- response

  model <- aov(response ~ treatment + block, data)
  anova_tbl <- anova(model) |> tidy()

  treatment_row <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "treatment")

  F_stat <- treatment_row$statistic
  p_value <- treatment_row$p.value
  reject_F <- p_value < 0.05

  tukey_reject <- TukeyHSD(model, "treatment")$treatment |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    pull(`p adj`) |>
    (\(x) any(x < 0.05))()

  R2 <- summary(lm(response ~ treatment + block, data))$r.squared

  MS_error <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "Residuals") |>
    pull(meansq)
  
  MS_treatment <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "treatment") |>
    pull(meansq)

  tibble(
    F_stat = F_stat,
    reject_F = reject_F,
    R2 = R2,
    tukey_reject = tukey_reject,
    MSE = MS_error,
    MSa = MS_treatment
  )
}
```

```{r}
# EXPERIMENT 2: HETEROSCEDASTIC (PROP TO MEAN) SIMULATIONS

set.seed(100)

results_normal1 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_mu2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "normal"))

results_lognormal1 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_mu2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "lognormal"))

results_log1 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_mu2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "log"))

results_exp1 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_mu2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "exp"))

results1 <- bind_rows(
  normal = results_normal1,
  lognormal = results_lognormal1,
  log = results_log1,
  exp = results_exp1,
  .id = "dataset"
) |>
  group_by(dataset) |>
  summarise(
    type1_error = mean(reject_F),
    mean_F = mean(F_stat),
    var_F = var(F_stat),
    mean_MSa = mean(MSa),
    var_MSa = var(MSa),
    mean_MSE = mean(MSE),
    var_MSE = var(MSE),
    mean_R2 = mean(R2),
    tukey_fwer = mean(tukey_reject)
  )
```

```{r}
# Plotting Empirical Type I Error Rates for Variance Proportional to Mean Matrix

ggplot(results1, aes(x = dataset, y = type1_error)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "red3") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Transformation",
    y = "Type I Error Rate",
    title = "Type I Error Rate for Normal Data With Transformations",
    subtitle = "Variance proportional to mean"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 0.08) +
  theme_bw()
```


```{r}
### 3. Heteroscedastic Simulation Model (Variance Proportional to Block Effect Squared)

R2_sim_b2 <- function(blocks,
                       treatments,
                       mu,
                       sigma_b,
                       R2_target,
                       response_type = "normal") {

  data <- expand.grid(
    block = factor(1:blocks),
    treatment = factor(1:treatments)
  )

  alpha_t <- rep(0, treatments)
  beta_b <- rnorm(blocks, 0, sigma_b)

  S <- treatments * sum((beta_b - mean(beta_b))^2)

  # CRITICAL STRUCTURAL CHANGE: Variance weight is defined purely by block components
  variance_matrix <- outer(
    alpha_t,
    beta_b,
    function(alpha, beta) beta^2
  )
  Vbar <- mean(variance_matrix)

  A1 <- treatments + blocks - 2
  B1 <- treatments * blocks - 1

  sigma <- sqrt(S * (1 - R2_target) /(Vbar * (R2_target * B1 - A1)))
  mu_tb <- mu + alpha_t[data$treatment] + beta_b[data$block]

  # Assign variance structural weights strictly proportional to beta^2
  V_tb <- beta_b[data$block]^2

  e <- rnorm(nrow(data), mean = 0, sd = sigma * sqrt(V_tb))
  y1 <- mu_tb + e

  Var_tb <- sigma^2 * V_tb
  a_tb <- sqrt(log(1 + Var_tb / mu_tb^2)) / sqrt(Var_tb)
  b_tb <- log(mu_tb) - a_tb * mu_tb - (a_tb^2 * Var_tb) / 2

  if (response_type == "normal") {
    response <- y1
  } else if (response_type == "lognormal") {
    response <- exp(a_tb * y1 + b_tb)
  } else if (response_type == "log") {
    response <- a_tb * y1 + b_tb
  } else if (response_type == "exp") {
    response <- exp(y1)
  }

  data$response <- response

  model <- aov(response ~ treatment + block, data)
  anova_tbl <- anova(model) |> tidy()

  treatment_row <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "treatment")

  F_stat <- treatment_row$statistic
  p_value <- treatment_row$p.value
  reject_F <- p_value < 0.05

  tukey_reject <- TukeyHSD(model, "treatment")$treatment |>
    as.data.frame() |>
    pull(`p adj`) |>
    (\(x) any(x < 0.05))()

  R2 <- summary(lm(response ~ treatment + block, data))$r.squared

  MS_error <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "Residuals") |>
    pull(meansq)

  MS_treatment <- anova_tbl |>
    filter(term == "treatment") |>
    pull(meansq)

  tibble(
    F_stat = F_stat,
    reject_F = reject_F,
    R2 = R2,
    tukey_reject = tukey_reject,
    MSE = MS_error,
    MSa = MS_treatment
  )
}
```

```{r}
# EXPERIMENT 3: HETEROSCEDASTIC (PROP TO BLOCK) SIMULATIONS

set.seed(100)

results_normal2 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_b2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "normal"))

results_lognormal2 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_b2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "lognormal"))

results_log2 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_b2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "log"))

results_exp2 <- map_dfr(1:10000, ~ R2_sim_b2(7, 8, 20, 3, R2_target, "exp"))

results2 <- bind_rows(
  normal = results_normal2,
  lognormal = results_lognormal2,
  log = results_log2,
  exp = results_exp2,
  .id = "dataset"
) |>
  group_by(dataset) |>
  summarise(
    type1_error = mean(reject_F),
    mean_F = mean(F_stat),
    var_F = var(F_stat),
    mean_MSa = mean(MSa),
    var_MSa = var(MSa),
    mean_MSE = mean(MSE),
    var_MSE = var(MSE),
    mean_R2 = mean(R2),
    tukey_fwer = mean(tukey_reject)
  )
```

```{r}
# Plotting Empirical Type I Error Rates for Variance Proportional to Block Variance

ggplot(results2, aes(x = dataset, y = type1_error)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "red3") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = "Transformation",
    y = "Type I Error Rate",
    title = "Type I Error Rate for Heteroscedastic Data With Transformations",
    subtitle = "Variance proportional to block effect"
  ) +
  ylim(0, 0.08) +
  theme_bw()
```


```{r}
# EXPORT DATA FOR TABLES / VISUALIZATIONS

write_csv(results, "homo_data.csv")
write_csv(results1, "hetero_mean_data.csv")
write_csv(results2, "hetero_beta_data.csv")
```

```{r}
# DISTRIBUTION VISUALIZATION GENERATION (DIAGNOSTIC HISTS)

sim_hist <- function(blocks,
                   treatments,
                   mu,
                   sigma_b,
                   R2_target) {

  data <- expand.grid(
    block = factor(1:blocks),
    treatment = factor(1:treatments)
  )

  alpha_t <- rep(0, treatments)
  
  beta_b <- rnorm(blocks, 0, sigma_b)

  S <- treatments * sum((beta_b - mean(beta_b))^2)

  Vbar <- 1

  A1 <- treatments + blocks - 2
  B1 <- treatments * blocks - 1

  sigma <- sqrt(S * (1 - R2_target) /(Vbar * (R2_target * B1 - A1)))

  mu_tb <- mu + alpha_t[data$treatment] + beta_b[data$block]

  normal <- mu_tb + rnorm(nrow(data), 0, sigma)

  lognormal <- rlnorm(
    nrow(data),
    meanlog = 3,
    sdlog = 0.5
  )

  exponential <- rexp(
    nrow(data),
    rate = 1 / 20)

  log_dist <- lognormal |> log()
  
  tibble(
    normal = normal,
    lognormal = lognormal,
    log = log_dist,
    exp = exponential
    )
}
```

```{r}
# Generate distribution diagnostics to verify simulated skewness profiles
normal_data <- sim_hist(20, 20, 20, 10, 0.5)

normal_hist    <- normal_data$normal
lognormal_hist <- normal_data$lognormal
log_hist       <- normal_data$log
exp_hist       <- normal_data$exp

# Render baseline diagnostics
hist(normal_hist, breaks = 50)
hist(lognormal_hist, breaks = 50)
hist(log_hist, breaks = 50)
hist(exp_hist, breaks = 50)
```
